findings.required_human_action: Check whether any of [Markus Anderljung] has an explicit sourced relationship to DSIT, ARIA, Dialog, Matt Clifford, or another spine node. If yes, add that row to the master doc. If no, flag as an island pending further research.

actors: Alex Cooper (fmr. DHSC/No. 10 COVID testing director)

findings.observed_pattern: 7 nodes (Alex Cooper (fmr. DHSC/No. 10 COVID testing director), Louis Mosley (Palantir EVP), Marc Andreessen…) share neighbors [Electric Twin] but have no path to the Clifford target.
findings.why_it_matters: These nodes are connected to each other but the cluster has no explicit ledger edge into the Clifford spine. The missing bridge is not inferred — it needs an explicit sourced row in the master doc.
findings.required_human_action: Check whether any of [Electric Twin] has an explicit sourced relationship to DSIT, ARIA, Dialog, Matt Clifford, or another spine node. If yes, add that row to the master doc. If no, flag as an island pending further research.

findings.observed_pattern: "Matt Clifford" (id: matt-clifford) and "Matt Clifford (Action Plan)" (id: matt-clifford-action-plan) may refer to the same actor. Their normalised labels overlap.
findings.why_it_matters: If these are the same entity, all edges pointing to the longer/variant id are invisible when searching for the canonical name. The Clifford Number for one may be different from the other purely as a compilation artifact.
findings.required_human_action: Confirm whether these are the same entity. If yes, add an alias entry in data/canonical/aliases.yml and ensure all edges use the canonical id. If no, add a note clarifying the distinction.

findings.observed_pattern: Corridor "No. 10 / Cabinet Office": ALL 3 known seed nodes are islands with no Clifford path.
findings.why_it_matters: Either the whole corridor is not yet connected to the spine, or the spine edges exist in the master doc but were not compiled (check compile-report.md).
findings.required_human_action: Review compile-report.md to confirm these nodes were created and their edges were compiled. If compiled, add an explicit spine connection from one corridor member to DSIT, ARIA, Dialog, or Matt Clifford.

findings.observed_pattern: 103 total island nodes. Sample: PsiQuantum, SpaceX, Groq, Renaissance Technologies, Jeremy O'Brien….
findings.why_it_matters: For each island, the question is not just "how does this connect" but "what kind of edge would this be": personnel, policy, funding, governance, advisory, or access? The type of edge determines what source is needed.
findings.required_human_action:
For each island cluster, answer:
1. Is this a personnel edge (same unit, same org, same role sequence)?
2. Is this a policy edge (same document, same recommendation, same adoption layer)?
3. Is this a funding edge (capital relationship, grant, procurement)?
4. Is this a governance edge (board, advisory, oversight)?
5. Is this an access edge (Dialog, event, programme, informal)?
6. Does this hop create a public path, or only explain a functional corridor?
Only add the edge when you have a sourced row that states the relationship you actually mean.
